threats.asset_or_claim: A Nominal result denotes a complete, exact-frame assessment rather than absence of input.
threats.actor: faulty producer, delayed broker, or accidental operator
threats.preconditions: one or more expected channels are missing, stale, delayed, gated, or from a different sequence
threats.sequence: partial evidence reaches the detector; readiness is confused with non-anomaly; a consumer treats the result as healthy
threats.trust_boundary: producer and lifecycle evidence to detector core
threats.observable_symptoms: unequal latest sequences, missing modality, insufficient samples, excess timestamp span, or an explicit miss
threats.worst_consequence: missing evidence is silently converted into reassuring evidence
threats.preventive_controls: exact-sequence readiness, explicit expected modalities, lifecycle completion, typed insufficiency, and fail-closed fusion
threats.detective_controls: mixed-sequence, missingness, stale-channel, and truth-table tests
threats.recovery: abstain, retain the last coherent suffix, and require a lifecycle reset or new epoch where continuity was broken
threats.residual_risk: all-modal silence is visible only when the independent heartbeat/deadline path is deployed correctly
threats.claim_impact: CLM-001 through CLM-003 are limited to implemented source behavior; operational availability remains NOT_CLAIMED

threats.preconditions: equal-dimensional vectors use different frames, units, projection algorithms, contexts, priors, tracks, or times
threats.sequence: metadata is omitted, aliased, or substituted; ordinal tails are compared; correlation is interpreted as physical consistency
threats.trust_boundary: producer projection and registry to core correlation
threats.observable_symptoms: projection identity mismatch, dimension mismatch, prior reuse, expired context, or absent projection
threats.worst_consequence: mathematically valid correlation is assigned a false physical meaning
threats.preventive_controls: typed projection identity, exact sequence alignment, pinned registry verification, and no native-residual fallback
threats.detective_controls: frame, context, unit-registry, stale-prior, and absent-projection negative tests
threats.recovery: return insufficient evidence or terminate the affected producer epoch
threats.residual_risk: producer attestation is a provenance claim, not proof of physical truth or correct calibration
threats.claim_impact: CLM-002 cannot be elevated to deployment qualification without authenticated external evidence

threats.asset_or_claim: Consistency evidence identifies truth, malicious intent, or every compromise.
threats.actor: coordinated compromised sensors or an overinterpreting consumer
threats.preconditions: several channels preserve the monitored marginal and dependence statistics
threats.sequence: the attack remains inside the configured statistical equivalence class; a report is interpreted causally
threats.trust_boundary: statistical report to human or downstream policy
threats.worst_consequence: a coordinated compromise is treated as truth or an innocent channel is blamed
threats.preventive_controls: advisory-only outcome vocabulary, calibrated_posterior=false, and explicit non-identifiability documentation
threats.detective_controls: consistency-preserving and collusion campaigns plus claim-language scanning
threats.recovery: use independent safety and authentication controls; do not widen authority from Galadriel output
threats.residual_risk: statistics-preserving attacks are fundamentally outside the selected estimands
threats.claim_impact: sensor truth, attack intent, and posterior probability are NOT_CLAIMED in CLM-006

threats.asset_or_claim: Accepted configuration and report values satisfy their invariants.
threats.actor: buggy caller, malicious serialized input, or future maintainer
threats.preconditions: public fields, unchecked construction, mutable accepted values, or validation-bypassing deserialization exist
threats.sequence: contradictory flags or impossible parameters are constructed; fusion trusts them; Nominal or excessive work follows
threats.trust_boundary: configuration/report input to statistical and runtime components
threats.observable_symptoms: invalid ranges, dormant Boolean payloads, contradictory verdict flags, or work-limit overflow
threats.worst_consequence: unsafe semantics or denial of service crosses a nominally validated API
threats.preventive_controls: private accepted types, strict raw parameter DTOs, fallible constructors, typed errors, immutable derivation, and validated outcomes
threats.detective_controls: forged-report, unknown-field, duplicate-key, boundary, and property tests
threats.recovery: reject before allocation or state mutation and preserve the previous accepted state
threats.residual_risk: the release remains blocked while any accepted configuration or decision boundary is still publicly forgeable
threats.claim_impact: implemented claims require the final configuration and report boundary audit to pass

threats.asset_or_claim: Retained samples form one contiguous producer generation.
threats.actor: replay attacker, lossy transport, restarted producer, or successes-only capture
threats.preconditions: sequence or timestamp gaps, regressions, duplicates, or epoch reuse occur
threats.sequence: old and new suffixes are combined or a hole is silently treated as a statistical reset
threats.trust_boundary: stream identity and lifecycle to detector state
threats.observable_symptoms: duplicate/regressing identity, advance greater than policy, timestamp regression, or prior reuse
threats.worst_consequence: non-contiguous evidence accumulates into an invalid nominal or calibrated-looking result
threats.preventive_controls: typed sequence/time identities, explicit ResetRequired, immutable retained suffix on rejection, and fresh epoch requirements
threats.detective_controls: gap, replay, timestamp, epoch, and historical-capture tests
threats.recovery: issue a lifecycle reset receipt or mint a new deployment epoch; never continue the old generation implicitly
threats.residual_risk: bounded replay ledgers require planned epoch rollover before their capacity is exhausted
threats.claim_impact: historical successes-only captures cannot establish stream calibration or availability

threats.asset_or_claim: Hostile but syntactically valid input cannot cause unbounded memory or compute.
threats.actor: unauthenticated peer, authorized abusive producer, or malicious fixture author
threats.preconditions: payload, record, track, modality, window, pair, grid, bootstrap, queue, or registry products are not bounded before work
threats.sequence: large cardinalities pass scalar checks; aggregate allocation or quadratic estimation begins
threats.trust_boundary: external bytes and research parameters to runtime allocation/work
threats.observable_symptoms: oversized payload, large product estimate, queue saturation, allocation failure, or prolonged callback
threats.worst_consequence: memory exhaustion, CPU denial of service, missed deadlines, or process termination
threats.preventive_controls: pre-parse byte ceilings where possible, checked aggregate arithmetic, fixed work caps, bounded queues, and optional heavy features
threats.detective_controls: exact-boundary, overflow, allocation-failure, saturation, and long-duration resource campaigns
threats.recovery: reject/drop with typed accounting, fault the affected epoch when integrity is uncertain, and require operator capacity review
threats.residual_risk: the Zenoh callback can materialize bytes before the application payload limit; broker limits remain mandatory
threats.claim_impact: platform deadlines and availability remain NOT_CLAIMED

threats.asset_or_claim: A route/session/producer claim is authenticated and authorized.
threats.actor: wrong-credential peer, compromised authorized peer, or misconfigured router
threats.preconditions: payload validation is mistaken for transport identity or ACL enforcement
threats.sequence: a valid-looking envelope is published on the wrong route or by the wrong identity and accepted as deployment evidence
threats.trust_boundary: Zenoh/router identity to NCP envelope provenance
threats.observable_symptoms: route, realm, session, producer, certificate, registry, or contract mismatch
threats.worst_consequence: untrusted evidence is attributed to an authorized producer
threats.preventive_controls: exact route and provenance validation, secure profile rendering, mTLS/ACL separation, and no cryptographic language for producer-attested projections
threats.detective_controls: wrong/no-certificate, wrong-route, provenance, and mixed-version campaigns
threats.recovery: stop the receiver epoch, rotate credentials/epoch, and preserve terminal health evidence
threats.residual_risk: 0.9.0 has no accepted external actual-router certificate/ACL campaign
threats.claim_impact: secured deployment and external producer authentication are NOT_CLAIMED

threats.asset_or_claim: Observed successful updates are an unbiased sample of sensor opportunities.
threats.actor: ordinary association/gating behavior, compromised producer, or researcher
threats.preconditions: rejections, misses, births, and censored candidates are omitted from the data
threats.sequence: successful-update-only observations are analyzed as if opportunities were complete
threats.trust_boundary: producer lifecycle ledger to statistical evaluation
threats.observable_symptoms: missing frame summary, candidate/outcome count mismatch, absent miss reasons, or historical JSONL without lifecycle
threats.preventive_controls: two-route lifecycle assembly, explicit miss/outcome accounting, pinned opportunity policy, and not-estimable dispositions
threats.detective_controls: ledger reconciliation, gating-censorship, missing-summary, and fixture omission tests
threats.recovery: classify the evidence as incomplete and collect an accepted lifecycle-complete stream
threats.residual_risk: producer-declared lifecycle can still be dishonest without independent physical ground truth
threats.claim_impact: recorded-stream and field calibration are NOT_CLAIMED

threats.asset_or_claim: Galadriel cannot grant or widen authority.
threats.actor: downstream integrator, compromised consumer, or rushed operator
threats.preconditions: advisory output is connected to allow, lease, TTL, limit, watchdog, or control decisions
threats.sequence: a nominal/anomalous report directly changes an authorization profile or synchronous control path
threats.observable_symptoms: deny-to-allow transition, increased limit/TTL/lease, watchdog mutation, or non-record-only effect
threats.worst_consequence: an experimental detector changes safety-critical authority
threats.preventive_controls: machine-tested non-widening validator, record-only initial contract, no controller credentials, and Haldir optionality
threats.detective_controls: authority transition truth-table and serialization tests
threats.recovery: disable the adapter, restore the independent baseline profile, and investigate the unauthorized transition
threats.residual_risk: a consumer that bypasses the validator is outside the release claim
threats.claim_impact: no downstream enforcement or Haldir integration is qualified in 0.9.0

threats.asset_or_claim: Statistical rates retain their stated interpretation under repeated use.
threats.preconditions: single-look alpha, overlapping windows, persistent CUSUM state, axes, channels, tracks, and repeated assessments are conflated
threats.sequence: a per-assessment threshold is advertised as a mission false-alarm guarantee without sequential calibration
threats.observable_symptoms: high episode rate, mission false-alarm probability, autocorrelation sensitivity, or tuning/evaluation leakage
threats.worst_consequence: unsupported operational performance and safety expectations
threats.preventive_controls: explicit estimands, per-family corrections, preregistration, development/holdout separation, and claim-tier limits
threats.detective_controls: serial-dependence, repeated-look, covariance-scale, and null calibration studies
threats.recovery: retain the research profile but remove operational rate claims until a justified sequential method passes holdout
threats.residual_risk: the current default has not met a preregistered mission-level acceptance threshold
threats.claim_impact: CLM-007 remains NOT_CLAIMED and tasks T096, T097, and T100 cannot be treated as operational qualification

threats.asset_or_claim: Numerical outputs are finite, stable, and correctly classified at extreme inputs.
threats.actor: malicious input, extreme but valid observation, or numerical implementation defect
threats.preconditions: tail probabilities, sums, variance, covariance, Fisher transforms, or estimator distances approach representational limits
threats.sequence: overflow, underflow, cancellation, degeneracy, or NaN changes an anomaly into an error or nominal result
threats.trust_boundary: validated numeric input to statistical decision
threats.observable_symptoms: non-finite intermediate, saturated sum, degenerate column, ill-conditioned covariance, or unstable tail
threats.worst_consequence: positive evidence disappears or classification changes across platforms
threats.preventive_controls: finite domains, scaled accumulation, direct upper tails, strict degeneracy gates, and typed numerical failure
threats.detective_controls: high-precision/oracle comparisons, endpoint properties, monotonicity tests, and cross-platform matrix
threats.recovery: return typed failure or insufficiency; never substitute a nominal value
threats.residual_risk: platform-level numerical equivalence and independent estimator review remain incomplete until final matrix evidence
threats.claim_impact: numerical claims are limited to exact tested domains and are not field calibration

threats.asset_or_claim: Concurrent ingress, shutdown, and restart preserve ordering and terminal faults.
threats.preconditions: routes deliver out of order, queues fill, receivers close, or callbacks race with terminal fault publication
threats.sequence: a later ready frame escapes after the first integrity fault or stale state survives restart
threats.trust_boundary: transport callbacks to bounded receiver/assembler state
threats.observable_symptoms: gap timer expiry, queue drop, first-fault change, post-close receipt, or generation reuse
threats.worst_consequence: evidence crosses a fault boundary or health reporting lies
threats.preventive_controls: serialized ingress, bounded reorder/handoff, first-terminal-fault latching, queue invalidation, and fresh sessions
threats.detective_controls: reorder, close, saturation, gap-deadline, cancellation, and restart tests
threats.recovery: stop the epoch, drain no further evidence, and restart with new session identity
threats.residual_risk: actual network/router scheduling and long-duration behavior remain unqualified
threats.claim_impact: component behavior may be IMPLEMENTED; external operational availability is NOT_CLAIMED

threats.asset_or_claim: Exact source, dependency, toolchain, and artifact semantics are bound to the reviewed candidate.
threats.actor: dependency compromise, build substitution, malicious artifact producer, or accidental regeneration
threats.preconditions: an unpinned source, mutable tag, ambient configuration, substituted advisory database, incomplete artifact validator, or unrecorded toolchain is used
threats.sequence: a clean-looking build resolves other code or a retained artifact carries bytes or semantics from another source graph
threats.trust_boundary: source, dependency, toolchain, and artifact-generation inputs to the signed qualification tier
threats.observable_symptoms: lockfile or source mismatch, checksum change, wrong receipt count, package-member drift, SBOM graph conflict, license-set drift, or advisory database mismatch
threats.worst_consequence: tests, supply-chain records, and release artifacts describe different programs or dependency graphs
threats.preventive_controls: Cargo.lock, exact Git revisions, the 16-key base environment, Rust and Cargo 1.89.0 workspace gates, Rust and Cargo 1.97.1 current-stable gates, and clean isolated tool state. The external RustSec source must use origin https://github.com/RustSec/advisory-db, commit f981d991604f3e7d4a0eb94e559cb3e5a94a6dc2, and tree 26bea0ac10667f826b5522a828a27861ae4b5287.
threats.detective_controls: Qualification schema v3 requires 22 command receipts and 15 two-run comparisons. Source and package members bind to the candidate tree. SBOM fields bind to validated Cargo metadata and Cargo.lock. The 382-package host-filtered license set requires 707 assignments, path-independent package-ID SHA-256 4d514cd4ce1e8b636396debb309dfe6d3847997b83263def0cdf596a96193665, and semantic SHA-256 4c6619d9403977a60e7cca82ce1446386934b8adacc71444504d753c9fce0fe7. Workspace package IDs use workspace+crates/{name}#{name}@{version}.
threats.recovery: invalidate the candidate, restore reviewed inputs, regenerate deterministically, and rerun every affected gate
threats.residual_risk: compiler, registry, Git hosting, and upstream trust require external controls. The license inventory is the recorded 382-package host-filtered subset of the 437-package graph. Same-host comparisons do not prove independent or cross-platform reproduction.
threats.claim_impact: artifact checks support the exact GitHub source-release tier only. They do not qualify a deployment, deployed binary, security property, permanent archive, or independent replication.

threats.asset_or_claim: Retained evidence is complete and cannot be cherry-picked or rebound to another candidate.
threats.actor: rushed researcher, malicious publisher, or storage fault
threats.preconditions: commands, failures, seeds, receipts, inputs, statuses, or hashes are omitted. The candidate authenticates itself, the tree is dirty, or uploaded tier bytes are rebound, replaced, or cherry-picked.
threats.sequence: only favorable summaries are retained, a later commit is referenced, or a candidate-controlled trust file authenticates an incomplete evidence tier
threats.trust_boundary: evaluation and build processes to authenticated release evidence and public claims
threats.observable_symptoms: missing raw trials or logs, another auxiliary receipt count, fewer than 13 mutation artifacts, dirty=true, unknown commit, checksum mismatch, or unexplained failed gate
threats.worst_consequence: unsupported claims appear reproducible and negative results disappear
threats.preventive_controls: an independently obtained allowed-signers file, complete command logs, 22 auxiliary receipts, 13 signed mutation artifacts, immutable inputs, raw results, manifest hashes, claim tiers, and explicit OPEN or NOT_CLAIMED statuses. Candidate commands use exact read, write, network, process, memory, file, and time bounds.
threats.detective_controls: qualification schema v3 validation, clean-tree refusal, exact receipt and mutation inventories, task ledger validation, final twenty-lens review, exact four-asset verification, and safe reconstruction against the independent trust root. Unique sandbox probes and a full process scan detect detached descendants.
threats.recovery: withdraw the evidence or release, publish the failure, and reproduce from a clean identified candidate
threats.residual_risk: 0.9.0 has no independent archive or DOI. Qualification is author-operated. macOS process discovery is not atomic. A short-lived reparented process can exit between scans. A sandboxed process can request work from an existing external service. The process scan cannot attribute that external service work. The packager excludes its signing-key handle but cannot classify a pre-existing secret copy inside an otherwise admitted finalized-tier file.
threats.claim_impact: GitHub source-release evidence is narrower and less durable than an independent archive. It is not independent replication or deployment qualification.

threats.asset_or_claim: Cross-repository contracts and published identities match the candidate.
threats.actor: concurrent maintainer, stale documentation, or incompatible upstream release
threats.preconditions: another repository advances after the audit pin or a source-branch version is described as a published artifact
threats.sequence: stale reciprocal commits, schema assumptions, or adapter semantics are advertised as current qualification
threats.trust_boundary: Galadriel to NCP, pid-rs, Crebain, Haldir, Prisoma, Engram/Paper2Brain, ROS / ROS 2, and external authority
threats.observable_symptoms: commit/schema/version mismatch, missing reciprocal manifest, mixed-version failure, or unreviewed concurrent change
threats.worst_consequence: an integration works only against a private snapshot while documentation promises a release contract
threats.preventive_controls: immutable pins, local convergence manifests, reciprocal requirement IDs, mixed-version tests, and truthful NOT_CLAIMED edges
threats.detective_controls: cross-repository reconciliation and exact revision checks
threats.recovery: remove the integration claim, update the pin through normal review, and rerun the affected matrix
threats.residual_risk: NCP 1.0 and a published pid-rs 1.x artifact are absent from the accepted 0.9 evidence

threats.asset_or_claim: Release identity, citation, tags, and support metadata describe exactly 0.9.0.
threats.preconditions: old tags/releases, stale versions, DOI/Zenodo placeholders, or production language remain
threats.sequence: the source tree and remote release metadata diverge or an identifier is claimed before it exists
threats.trust_boundary: candidate source and evidence to GitHub/citation users
threats.observable_symptoms: multiple current tags, wrong Cargo/CFF version, old release asset, DOI field, or crates.io/production promise
threats.worst_consequence: users cite or depend on the wrong artifact and later evidence cannot identify it
threats.preventive_controls: single signed v0.9.0 tag, withdrawn-release record, metadata audit, publish=false, explicit null DOI/Zenodo fields, a signed two-tar asset map with exact four-file upload-set verification, and draft-first publication without replacement
threats.detective_controls: release audit, remote tag/release enumeration, exact authenticated draft downloads, safe reconstruction, anonymous post-publication downloads, and tagged-link verification
threats.recovery: execute the documented withdrawal procedure, remove misleading remote objects, correct metadata, and republish only from an unchanged verified candidate
threats.residual_risk: tag-based URLs rely on preserving the signed tag without deletion or replacement; DOI and Zenodo preservation are intentionally deferred until the author creates them
